rulings.Case Title: DELORIS TURNER, et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 31, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:00 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declaration of Rudy Limpert creates a triable issue whether defendant is liable for Mr. Turner¿s alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant¿s Ammco brake grinding machine. While such machine itself did not contain asbestos or asbestos-containing component parts, there is a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Turner. Plaintiffs¿ objections to the declarations of Craig Mountz and Russell Darnell are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to the declaration of Rudy Limpert are overruled. Plaintiffs¿ request for a continuance pursuant to C.C.P. 437c(h) is denied as irrelevant. Whether Mr. Turner was exposed to asbestos from an Ammco brake grinding machine, the basis for plaintiffs¿ request, was not a ground upon which defendant moved for summary judgment or adjudication. If a hearing is requested, it will be 9:00a.m., not 9:30a.m. Due to the court¿s trial schedule all motion hearings must conclude no later than 9:25a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GARY KASE et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 31, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Fraser¿s Boiler Service Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)
